turns.note: Auto-selected 07_flow_matching_notes.pdf with source_mode=file; interpolant X_t = tX_1+(1−t)X_0 (t=0 noise, t=1 data) and target velocity v = X_1−X_0 both match the notes.
VE drift f=0 and VP drift f=−½β(t)x_t are correct with f/g roles identified, but it writes the VE-SDE as dx=σ(t)dW_t where the notes have dx=√(dσ²(t)/dt)dW_t.

Predictor written as x_{i−1}=x_i−[f−½g²S_θ]Δt+g·Z_i — the ½ belongs only to the probability-flow ODE per the notes' trap warning, and the √Δt scaling on the noise is dropped; the Langevin corrector is also given with a wrong sign (x←x−αS_θ+√(2α)Z); the notes' ODE-halves-score/drops-noise contrast is never mentioned.

Only an answer key is delivered (5 answers, all supported by the notes: v=X_1−X_0, VE f=0, VP drift, predictor components) — the quiz questions themselves never appear, and it claims 'the quiz card is now ready in your panel' while the transcript records no suggestion and no artifact.
Responds only 'the quiz is generated and ready to publish' — no student version is produced anywhere in the transcript (nothing leaked, but nothing delivered either).

Names the right file (07_flow_matching_notes.pdf) but contradicts the user by claiming 'the file you attached to this message' after the user said they never picked one, and leaks internal prompt scaffolding ('<selected_course_sources> block', 'FOCUS').

highlight: Auto-selection and turns 1-2 were solid, but the predictor-corrector answer commits the exact trap the notes warn about (½g² in the Euler-Maruyama step, no √Δt), turns 4-5 claim quiz cards that don't exist in the transcript, and turn 6 tells the user they attached the file while exposing prompt internals.
